svg.text: SINHALA
HISTORY & INDIAN OCEAN CONNECTIONS
SINHALA ORIGINS
~500 BCE: Prince Vijaya from North India arrives in Lanka
Mahavamsa: Sanskrit chronicle; Sinhala identity tied to Buddhism
247 BCE: Buddhism arrives in Lanka (Mahinda, son of Ashoka)
Sinhala became the language of Theravada Buddhist scholarship
Anuradhapura (377 BCE-1017 CE): ancient Sinhala capital
SINHALA LITERATURE
~2nd c. BCE: earliest Sinhala inscriptions at rock caves
Sigiri graffiti (5th-8th c. CE): earliest Sinhala poetry
Mahavamsa (~5th c. CE): Buddhist historical epic
~10M speakers; official language of Sri Lanka
MALAYALAM HISTORY · SECONDARY LANGUAGE
KERALA — SPICE COAST
Kerala: world’s most sought pepper, cardamom, ginger, nutmeg
Arab, Chinese, Greek, Roman traders all visited Malayalam coast
~830 CE: Malayalam separates from Tamil as distinct language
Cheraman Perumals: last kings united Kerala before fragmentation
MALAYALAM LITERARY TRADITION
Ramacharitam (12th c.): first major Malayalam literary text
Manipravalam: mixed Sanskrit-Malayalam literary style
Ezhuthachan (16th c.): father of modern Malayalam literature
Thunchaththu Ramanujan Ezhuthachan: adapted Ramayana in Malayalam
KERALA TODAY
~38M speakers; official language of Kerala state
Kerala: 100% literacy rate — highest in India
Malayalam diaspora: Gulf countries, USA, UK
THE PALK STRAIT CONNECTION
Sri Lanka and Kerala separated by ~240mi of ocean
Trade: cinnamon (Lanka) − pepper (Kerala) — ancient exchange
Sinhala absorbed Dravidian phonology from Tamil/Malayalam contact
Malayalam loanwords in Sinhala: hundreds of common terms
Buddhist monks: Malayalam Kerala ↔ Sri Lanka exchange routes
Two island/coastal peoples shaped by the Indian Ocean
PLATE 44d ALT · SINHALA / MALAYALAM · HISTORY
